attacking_style: Attacks from breakaways on final classified climbs 35-45km out, but finishes races on descents and flat finales rather than summit finishes. Shows patience to track moves rather than initiate early.
team_tactics: UAE provides limited domestique support in steep finales, forcing solo efforts. Functions as stage hunter from breaks rather than protected GC leader, though 2023 signals internal hierarchy shift over Yates.
terrain_preference: Climbing time trials and lumpy technical courses suit best. Descending is stronger weapon than pure climbing. Exposed on summit finishes requiring sustained steep gradient efforts and vulnerable in echelon/wind splits.
vulnerability: Positioning deficiencies in bunch racing lead to losses in crashes and wind splits during transitional stages. Cannot sustain attacks on steep gradients without team support. White jersey/GC pressure increases crash risk.
key_patterns: Time trial specialist among climbers: beat Vingegaard at intermediate, only 6s slower than Ganna (2 observations)
Descending closes gaps climbing opens: caught Healy descending twice for stage wins (2 observations)
Positional weakness costs GC: lost time in echelons and crashes on transitional stages (3 observations)
Breakaway finisher not peloton sprinter: wins from small groups 35-45km out, not bunch kicks (3 observations)
Matured from GC prospect to stage hunter: abandoned white jersey defense, now targets opportunistic wins (4 observations)
form_trajectory: positive: progression from crash abandons (2021) to first Grand Tour stage win (2023) shows maturation. UAE hierarchy shift over Yates signals confidence growth and tactical intelligence improvement.
